title: silene fruticosa subsp. cyrenaica
intro: Silene fruticosa subsp. cyrenaica, also known as Cyrenaic catchfly, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Caryophyllaceae. It is a perennial herb native to the Mediterranean region, with medicinal properties attributed to its leaves and flowers. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Herbal preparations of Silene fruticosa subsp. cyrenaica can be made into infusions, decoctions, or infusions, which are often used to alleviate symptoms of colds, sore throats, and respiratory issues.
